sections.label: Section 1060c
sections.original:
ναί , εὕδει
ὕπνον γʼ ἄυπνον ὀλόμενον , ὃς ἔκανεν ἄλοχον ,
ἔκανε δὲ τέκεα , τοξήρει ψαλμῷ τοξεύσας .
sections.translation: Yes , he is sleeping , a deadly sleepless sleep , having slain wife and children with the arrows of his twanging bow .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: στέναζέ νυν —
sections.translation: Ah ! mourn —

sections.label: Section 1065b
sections.original: στενάζω .
sections.translation: Indeed I do .

sections.label: Section 1066
sections.original: τέκνων ὄλεθρον —
sections.translation: The children ’ s death —

sections.label: Section 1066b
sections.original: ὤμοι .
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1067
sections.original:
σέθεν τε παιδὸς —
αἰαῖ .
sections.translation: And your own son ’ s doom .

sections.label: Section 1067b
sections.original: ὦ πρέσβυ ⋯
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 1068
sections.original: σῖγα σῖγα ·
sections.translation: Old friend —

sections.label: Section 1068b
sections.original: παλίντροπος ἐξεγειρόμενος στρέφεται · φέρε ,
sections.translation: Hush ! hush ! he is turning over , he is waking ! Oh !

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἀπόκρυφον δέμας ὑπὸ μέλαθρον κρύψω .
sections.translation: let me hide myself , concealed beneath the roof .

sections.label: Section 1071
sections.original: θάρσει · νὺξ ἔχει βλέφαρα παιδὶ σῷ .
sections.translation: Courage ! darkness still holds your son ’ s eye .

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original:
ὁρᾶθʼ ὁρᾶτε . τὸ φάος ἐκ-
λιπεῖν μὲν ἐπὶ κακοῖσιν οὐ
φεύγω τάλας , ἀλλʼ εἴ με κανεῖ πατέρʼ ὄντα ,
sections.translation: Oh beware ! it is not that I shrink from leaving the light after my miseries , poor wretch ! but if should he slay me , his father ,

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original:
πρὸς δὲ κακοῖς κακὰ μήσεται
πρὸς Ἐρινύσι θʼ αἷμα σύγγονον ἕξει .
sections.translation: then he will be devising mischief on mischief , and to the avenging curse will add a parent ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 1078
sections.original:
τότε θανεῖν σʼ ἐχρῆν , ὅτε δάμαρτι σᾷ
φόνον ὁμοσπόρων ἔμολες ἐκπράξας
sections.translation: Well for you if you had died in that day , when , for your wife , you went forth to exact vengeance for her slain brothers

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: Ταφίων περίκλυστον ἄστυ πέρσας .
sections.translation: by sacking the Taphians ’ sea-beat town .

sections.label: Section 1081
sections.original:
φυγὰν φυγάν , γέροντες , ἀποπρὸ δωμάτων
διώκετε · φεύγετε μάργον
ἄνδρʼ ἐπεγειρόμενον .
ἢ τάχα φόνον ἕτερον ἐπὶ φόνῳ βαλὼν
sections.translation: Fly , fly , my aged friends , from before the palace , escape his waking fury . Or soon he will heap up fresh slaughter on the old ,

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ἀνʼ αὖ βακχεύσει Καδμείων πόλιν .
sections.translation: ranging wildly once more through the streets of Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1086
sections.original:
ὦ Ζεῦ , τί παῖδʼ ἤχθηρας ὧδʼ ὑπερκότως
τὸν σόν , κακῶν δὲ πέλαγος ἐς τόδʼ ἤγαγες ;
sections.translation: O Zeus , why have you shown such savage hate against your own son and plunged him in this sea of troubles ?

sections.label: Section 1088
sections.original:
ἔα ·
ἔμπνους μέν εἰμι καὶ δέδορχʼ ἅπερ με δεῖ ,
sections.translation: Aha ! I am alive and breathing ; and my eyes resume their function , opening on

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original:
αἰθέρα τε καὶ γῆν τόξα θʼ Ἡλίου τάδε ⋯
ὡς ἐν κλύδωνι καὶ φρενῶν ταράγματι
πέπτωκα δεινῷ καὶ πνοὰς θερμὰς πνέω
μετάρσιʼ , οὐ βέβαια , πνευμόνων ἄπο .
ἰδού , τί δεσμοῖς ναῦς ὅπως ὡρμισμένος
νεανίαν θώρακα καὶ βραχίονα ,
sections.translation: the sky and earth and the sun ’ s darting beam ; but how my senses reel ! in what strange turmoil am I plunged ! my fevered breath in quick spasmodic gasps escapes my lungs . How now ? why am I lying here , my brawny chest and arms made fast with cables like a ship ,

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original:
πρὸς ἡμιθραύστῳ λαΐνῳ τυκίσματι
ἧμαι , νεκροῖσι γείτονας θάκους ἔχων ;
πτερωτά τʼ ἔγχη · τόξα δʼ ἔσπαρται πέδῳ ,
ἃ πρὶν παρασπίζοντʼ ἐμοῖς βραχίοσιν
sections.translation: beside a half-shattered piece of masonry , with corpses for my neighbors ; while over the floor my bow and arrows are scattered , that once like trusty squires to my arm

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original:
ἔσῳζε πλευρὰς ἐξ ἐμοῦ τʼ ἐσῴζετο .
οὔ που κατῆλθον αὖθις εἰς Ἅιδου πάλιν ,
Εὐρυσθέως δίαυλον ; εἰς Ἅιδου ; πόθεν ;
ἀλλʼ οὔτε Σισύφειον εἰσορῶ πέτρον
Πλούτωνά τʼ , οὐδὲ σκῆπτρα Δήμητρος κόρης .
sections.translation: both kept me safe and were kept safe by me ? Surely I have not come a second time to Hades ’ halls , having just returned from there for Eurystheus ? To Hades ? From where ? No , I do not see Sisyphus with his stone , or Pluto , or his queen , Demeter ’ s child .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.translation: Surely I am distraught ; where am I , so helpless ? Ho , there ! which of my friends is near or far to cure me in my perplexity ? For I have no clear knowledge of things once familiar .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: γέροντες , ἔλθω τῶν ἐμῶν κακῶν πέλας ;
sections.translation: My aged friends , shall I approach the scene of my sorrow ?

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: κἀγώ γε σὺν σοί , μὴ προδοὺς τὰς συμφοράς .
sections.translation: Yes , and let me go with you , not desert you in your trouble .

sections.label: Section 1111
sections.original:
πάτερ , τί κλαίεις καὶ συναμπίσχῃ κόρας ,
τοῦ φιλτάτου σοι τηλόθεν παιδὸς βεβώς ;
sections.translation: Father , why do you weep and veil your eyes , standing far from your beloved son ?

sections.label: Section 1113
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον · εἶ γὰρ καὶ κακῶς πράσσων ἐμός .
sections.translation: My child ! mine still , for all your misery .

sections.label: Section 1114
sections.original: πράσσω δʼ ἐγὼ τί λυπρόν , οὗ δακρυρροεῖς ;
sections.translation: Why , what is there so sad in my case that you weep ?

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: ἃ κἂν θεῶν τις , εἰ μάθοι , καταστένοι .
sections.translation: That which might make any of the gods weep , if he were to learn it .

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: μέγας γʼ ὁ κόμπος , τὴν τύχην δʼ οὔπω λέγεις .
sections.translation: A bold assertion that , but you are not yet explaining what has happened .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: ὁρᾷς γὰρ αὐτός , εἰ φρονῶν ἤδη κυρεῖς .
sections.translation: Your own eyes see that , if by this time you are restored to your senses .

sections.label: Section 1118
sections.original: εἴπʼ , εἴ τι καινὸν ὑπογράφῃ τὠμῷ βίῳ .
sections.translation: Fill in your sketch if any change awaits my life .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: εἰ μηκέθʼ Ἅιδου βάκχος εἶ , φράσαιμεν ἄν .
sections.translation: I will explain , if you are no longer mad as a fiend of hell .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: παπαῖ , τόδʼ ὡς ὕποπτον ᾐνίξω πάλιν .
sections.translation: Oh ! what suspicions these dark hints of yours again excite !

sections.label: Section 1121
sections.original: καί σʼ εἰ βεβαίως εὖ φρονεῖς ἤδη σκοπῶ .
sections.translation: I am still doubtful whether you are in your sober senses .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι βακχεύσας γε μέμνημαι φρένας .
sections.translation: I have no recollection of being mad .

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: λύσω , γέροντες , δεσμὰ παιδός ; ἢ τί δρῶ ;
sections.translation: Am I to loose my son , old friends , or what shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: καὶ τόν γε δήσαντʼ εἴπʼ · ἀναινόμεσθα γάρ .
sections.translation: Loose me , yes , and say who bound me ; for I feel shame at this .

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: τοσοῦτον ἴσθι τῶν κακῶν · τὰ δʼ ἄλλʼ ἔα .
sections.translation: Rest content with what you know of your woes ; the rest forego .

sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original: ἀρκεῖ σιωπὴ γὰρ μαθεῖν ὃ βούλομαι ;
sections.translation: No . for is silence sufficient to learn what I wish ?

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , παρʼ Ἥρας ἆρʼ ὁρᾷς θρόνων τάδε ;
sections.translation: O Zeus , do you behold these deeds proceeding from the throne of Hera ?

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ τι κεῖθεν πολέμιον πεπόνθαμεν ;
sections.translation: What ! have I suffered something from her enmity ?

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: τὴν θεὸν ἐάσας τὰ σὰ περιστέλλου κακά .
sections.translation: A truce to the goddess ! attend to your own troubles .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθα · συμφορὰν λέξεις τινά .
sections.translation: I am undone ; you will tell me some mischance .

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: ἰδού , θέασαι τάδε τέκνων πεσήματα .
sections.translation: See here the corpses of your children .

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: οἴμοι · τίνʼ ὄψιν τήνδε δέρκομαι τάλας ;
sections.translation: O horror ! what sight is here ? ah me !

sections.label: Section 1133
sections.original: ἀπόλεμον , ὦ παῖ , πόλεμον ἔσπευσας τέκνοις .
sections.translation: My son , against your children you have waged unnatural war .

sections.label: Section 1134
sections.original: τί πόλεμον εἶπας ; τούσδε τίς διώλεσε ;
sections.translation: War ! what do you mean ? who killed these ?

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: σὺ καὶ σὰ τόξα καὶ θεῶν ὃς αἴτιος .
sections.translation: You and your bow and some god , whoever is to blame .

sections.label: Section 1136
sections.original: τί φῄς ; τί δράσας ; ὦ κάκʼ ἀγγέλλων πάτερ .
sections.translation: What are you saying ? what have I done ? Speak , father , you messenger of evil !

sections.label: Section 1137
sections.original: μανείς · ἐρωτᾷς δʼ ἄθλιʼ ἑρμηνεύματα .
sections.translation: You were insane ; it is a sad explanation you are asking .

sections.label: Section 1138
sections.original: ἦ καὶ δάμαρτός εἰμʼ ἐγὼ φονεὺς ἐμῆς ;
sections.translation: Was it I that slew my wife also ?

sections.label: Section 1139
sections.original: μιᾶς ἅπαντα χειρὸς ἔργα σῆς τάδε .
sections.translation: Your own unaided arm has done all this .

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: αἰαῖ · στεναγμῶν γάρ με περιβάλλει νέφος .
sections.translation: Alas ! a cloud of mourning wraps me round .

sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original: τούτων ἕκατι σὰς καταστένω τύχας .
sections.translation: For this reason I lament your fate .

sections.label: Section 1142
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ συνήραξʼ οἶκον ἢ βάκχευσʼ ἐμόν ;
sections.translation: Did I dash my house to pieces in my frenzy ?

sections.label: Section 1143
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα πλὴν ἕν · πάντα δυστυχεῖ τὰ σά .
sections.translation: I know nothing but this , that you are utterly undone .

sections.label: Section 1144
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ οἶστρος ἡμᾶς ἔλαβε ; ποῦ διώλεσεν ;
sections.translation: Where did the madness seize me ? where did it destroy me ?

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: ὅτʼ ἀμφὶ βωμὸν χεῖρας ἡγνίζου πυρί .
sections.translation: When you were purifying yourself with fire at the altar .

sections.label: Section 1146
sections.original:
οἴμοι · τί δῆτα φείδομαι ψυχῆς ἐμῆς
τῶν φιλτάτων μοι γενόμενος παίδων φονεύς ;
κοὐκ εἶμι πέτρας λισσάδος πρὸς ἅλματα
ἢ φάσγανον πρὸς ἧπαρ ἐξακοντίσας
sections.translation: Ah me ! why do I spare my own life when I have become the murderer of my dear children ? Shall I not hasten to leap from some sheer rock , or aim the sword against my heart
